FluentGpu
Table of Contents

Class CaptionButton

Namespace
FluentGpu.Controls
Assembly
FluentGpu.Controls.dll

An engine-drawn window caption button (minimize / maximize-restore / close) for the custom-frame TitleBar — the Win11 shell look: 46×bar-height, square-edged (caption buttons abut the window edge, no corner radius), a 10px Segoe Fluent chrome glyph, subtle hover/press fills (close = the shell red with a white glyph). The OS owns those pixels' hit-testing (WM_NCHITTEST returns HTMINBUTTON/HTMAXBUTTON/HTCLOSE so the Win11 snap-layouts flyout works); the Win32 PAL synthesizes pointer input back into the engine from the WM_NC* mouse messages, so these ramps run through the normal InteractionAnimator path even though the pixels are non-client. Visual states = BoxEl fill ramp + TextEl foreground ramp at 83ms (WinUI ControlFasterAnimationDuration) with the BoxEl default (0,0,0,1) spline — never a per-control state machine.

public static class CaptionButton

Width

Field Value

float

Properties

Close

Close ramp: the Win11 shell red on hover/press with a white glyph (pressed glyph at ~70%).

public static CaptionButton.Style Close { get; }

Property Value

CaptionButton.Style

MinMax

Minimize/maximize ramp: transparent → SubtleSecondary → SubtleTertiary fills; glyph primary → secondary.

public static CaptionButton.Style MinMax { get; }

Property Value

CaptionButton.Style

Methods

Create(string, Action, Style, bool, float, Action<NodeHandle>?)

active = window activation (false dims the glyph to InactiveForeground — fills stay wired; Win11 shows hover states on inactive captions too). height = the bar height (caption buttons stretch the full bar, flush to the top edge). onRealized captures the node handle for the TitleBar's WM_NCHITTEST region report.

public static BoxEl Create(string glyph, Action onClick, CaptionButton.Style style, bool active = true, float height = 48, Action<NodeHandle>? onRealized = null)

Parameters

glyph string
onClick Action
style CaptionButton.Style
active bool
height float
onRealized Action<NodeHandle>

Returns

BoxEl
FluentGpu — a near-zero-allocation, NativeAOT, GPU-rendered UI engine for .NET 10. Usage docs live here; architecture canon lives in design/ (SPEC-INDEX.md is the precedence authority).
